transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Sunrise Recovery Center, Jennifer speaking.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Hi, I'm Sara. I'm looking at treatment options for alcohol. I've been drinking pretty heavily.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: How long?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: About two years. It's every day.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: How much a day?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Half a bottle of vodka, sometimes more.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Any withdrawal when you try to stop?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Some shaking. Nothing too bad.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Have you looked into what kind of program you want?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: I was thinking outpatient. I have a job and I can't just disappear.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Based on what you're describing — daily drinking, half a bottle of vodka, withdrawal symptoms — I think you need residential, not outpatient. Outpatient isn't going to be enough for where you are.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Oh. I didn't think it was that bad.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: It is. You should really consider the 30-day program. That's the right fit for your situation.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: I'd have to figure out my job. That's a big deal.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: FMLA can cover that. Do you have insurance?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Yes. NovaCare Health. ID BP62051326, group GRP-855615.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Okay. We'll verify. Someone will be in touch.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Do you know when?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Probably today.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Okay. Thank you.

gold_labels.coaching_notes: COMPLIANCE VIOLATION: 'I think you need residential, not outpatient' and 'Outpatient isn't going to be enough for where you are' are scope-of-practice violations — level-of-care determination requires clinical assessment, not intake staff judgment. Also: 'That's the right fit for your situation' is a clinical prescription. Agent should say 'our clinical team will assess the right level of care.' Beyond compliance: no empathy, objection to job impact dismissed with FMLA mention only, next step vague.
